hero.title: Your books in order, your deadlines met
hero.subtitle: Bookkeeping, payroll, and tax filings for entrepreneurs and small companies — handled quietly and on time, explained in plain language whenever you ask.

services.title: What we handle
services.subtitle: Everything between your invoices and the tax office.

items.title: Bookkeeping
items.description: Complete double-entry bookkeeping for companies and simplified books for entrepreneurs, kept current weekly — not reconstructed in a panic each January.

items.title: Payroll
items.description: Salaries, contributions, sick leave, and vacation calculations, delivered on the same day every month with slips your employees can actually read.

items.title: Tax filings & compliance
items.description: VAT, corporate and personal income tax, all filed before the deadline — you'll never learn about an obligation from a penalty notice.

items.title: Company formation
items.description: Choosing between entrepreneur and LLC, registration paperwork, and the tax setup that fits how you'll actually operate.

items.title: Reports & analysis
items.description: Monthly overviews that tell you how the business is really doing, in numbers you don't need an economics degree to read.

items.title: Advisory
items.description: A phone call before a big decision — new employee, new car, new market — so the tax consequences are a plan, not a surprise.

about.title: The office
about.subtitle: Small enough to know your business, experienced enough to protect it.
about.body: Bilans was founded in 2011 by Vesna Ilić after fifteen years in corporate finance. Today four of us look after around ninety clients — mostly entrepreneurs, agencies, and family firms. We deliberately stay small: every client has one accountant who knows their history, answers their calls, and notices when something in the numbers looks off before it becomes a problem. Deadlines are our obsession; in fourteen years we have not missed one.

items.title: One accountant, your accountant
items.description: The person who does your books is the person who answers your questions.

items.title: Plain language
items.description: Every answer comes with the 'what this means for you' translation.

items.title: Deadlines without reminders
items.description: We track every obligation; you hear from us before the state does.

items.title: Digital by default
items.description: Send documents by photo or email — no shoebox of receipts required.

items.title: Fixed monthly fee
items.description: One predictable price, agreed in writing, no per-question billing.

items.title: Proactive warnings
items.description: If a rule changes and affects you, you'll know the week it's announced.

items.name: Nikola V.
items.text: I switched after my old accountant surprised me with a tax bill he'd known about for months. Bilans calls me in advance about things I didn't even know existed. The difference is night and day.

items.name: Pekara Zlatno Zrno
items.text: Eleven years, three inspections, zero findings. Vesna knows our business better than we know it ourselves.

items.name: Ana S.
items.text: Payroll arrives the same morning every month like clockwork, and when I call with a dumb question I get an answer, not a lecture.

pricing.title: Monthly packages
pricing.subtitle: Fixed fees agreed in writing. The exact price depends on document volume — these are typical ranges.

plans.name: Entrepreneur (flat-rate)
plans.description: For flat-rate entrepreneurs: filings, deadlines, and advisory.

plans.name: Entrepreneur (books)
plans.description: Full books for entrepreneurs, with payroll for up to three employees.

plans.name: Company (LLC)
plans.description: Double-entry bookkeeping, payroll, VAT, and year-end statements.

items.question: I'm just starting out. Entrepreneur or LLC?
items.answer: It depends on your expected income, clients, and risk — there's no universal answer, and anyone who gives you one without asking questions is guessing. Bring your plan to a free consultation and you'll leave with a concrete recommendation and the numbers behind it.

items.question: How does switching from my current accountant work?
items.answer: You sign one authorization, and we handle the takeover directly with your current office — books, records, and open items. Switching mid-year is routine; the only bad time to switch is after a problem, not before one.

items.question: How do I get documents to you?
items.answer: Photograph or scan them and send by email or message as they arrive — no monthly shoebox delivery. If you prefer paper, our office is in the centre and there's a folder with your name on it.

items.question: What happens if there's an inspection?
items.answer: We attend it with you — or instead of you, with authorization. Since the books are kept properly all year, inspections at our clients are usually short and uneventful. That's rather the point of us.

cta.title: Talk to an accountant before you need one
cta.subtitle: The first consultation is free and obligation-free — bring your questions.

contact.title: Contact the office
contact.subtitle: Call, write, or come by — we're in the centre, second floor.
